expectations.evidence: Without the skill, naive Claude does not know Conduction's 8 Dutch government personas. It would write a generic summary of test findings, not a sectioned report broken out by Henk Bakker, Fatima, Sem, Noor, Annemarie, Mark, Priya, and Jan-Willem.

Cross-persona patterns require a prior multi-persona parallel run to produce comparison data. Without the skill's Step 2, there are no 8 separate persona result sets to aggregate into a cross-persona section.

expectations.text: Report uses structured COUNSEL_TEST_RESULT output line
expectations.evidence: The COUNSEL_TEST_RESULT: PASS | FAIL  CRITICAL_COUNT: <n>  SUMMARY: <one-line> format is skill-defined. Naive Claude would produce a narrative summary or a simple pass/fail statement, not a structured machine-parseable output line in this exact format.

expectations.text: Offers to create OpenSpec changes for discovered gaps
expectations.evidence: OpenSpec is a Conduction-internal specification workflow (openspec/changes/, openspec/specs/). Naive Claude is not aware of this system and would not know to offer creating OpenSpec changes as the follow-up action for test gaps.

notes: All four report format behaviors are Conduction-specific: persona sections, cross-persona aggregation, the COUNSEL_TEST_RESULT output format, and OpenSpec gap creation. General-purpose testing would produce none of these.
